revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Afukukahaku-jinja]][[ja:安福河伯神社]] {{Expand Japanese|安福河伯神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Afuku Kahaku Shrine | native_name = 安福河伯神社 | image = Haiden of Afuku Kahaku-jinja shrine.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Afuku Kahaku Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|38.076368|140.84286}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|fr|Hayakitsuhiko et Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|ko|하야아키츠히코・하야아키츠히메|simple|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|zh|速秋津日子神與速秋津比賣神|qid=Q11326733|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}} | established = {'time': '+0111-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} }} {{nihongo|'''Afuku Kahaku Shrine'''|安福河伯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 27 ** P155: {{ill|Kashimawonatano Shrine|lt=Kashimawonatano Shrine|qid=Q135039995|1=Kashimawonatano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11677110|ltq=Q11677110|qid=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Watari, Miyagi|lt=Watari|arz|بلده واتاراى|ceb|Watari Chō|de|Watari (Miyagi)|en|Watari, Miyagi|es|Watari (Miyagi)|fa|واتاری، میاگی|fr|Watari|id|Watari, Miyagi|it|Watari|ja|亘理町|ko|와타리정|mg|Watari|ms|Watari, Miyagi|my|ဝတရိမြို့၊ မိယဂိ|pl|Watari|ru|Ватари (посёлок)|sv|Watari|tl|Watari, Miyagi|tr|Watari|uk|Ватарі (Міяґі)|vi|Watari, Miyagi|zh_yue|亘理町|zh|亘理町|qid=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}} * {{ill|Q907389|ltq=Q907389|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} * {{ill|Watari District, Miyagi|lt=Watari district|ceb|Watari-gun|en|Watari District, Miyagi|fr|District de Watari|id|Distrik Watari, Miyagi|it|Distretto di Watari|ja|亘理郡|ko|와타리군|pl|Powiat Watari|ru|Ватари (уезд)|sr|Област Ватари|uk|Повіт Ватарі|zh_yue|亘理郡|zh|亘理郡|qid=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|38.076368|140.84286}} == inception (P571) == * {'time': '+0111-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} == image (P18) == * Haiden of Afuku Kahaku-jinja shrine.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q11326733|ltq=Q11326733|qid=Q11326733|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182064 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * あふくかはくじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4370805000162 == Commons category (P373) == * Afukukahaku-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県亘理郡亘理町逢隈田沢字堰下220', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Afukukahaku-jinja}} [[JA:安福河伯神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11451153}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Afuku Kahaku Shrine |Image=[[File:Haiden of Afuku Kahaku-jinja shrine.JPG|270px|Afuku Kahaku Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|qid=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|qid=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Watari Town|Q1205296|qid=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}} Okuma Tazawa, Aza Sekishita 220 |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates2 degrees minutes seconds|38|4|34.8|N|140|50|33.6|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko・Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|qid=Q11326733|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|qid=Q30640|1=AD 111}}) |Main hall style={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|qid=Q2503|1=April 8}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Afuku
Kahaku Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Afuku Kahaku Shrine''' (あふくかはくじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Watari Town|Q1205296|qid=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|qid=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|qid=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a gōsha. It is one of the four seats of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|qid=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deities === * The principal enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko・Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|qid=Q11326733|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}}, a god of the Abukuma River, together with {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Ōkami|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}, and {{ill|lt=Toshigami|Q3028663|qid=Q3028663|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|1=Toshigami}}. ** In addition, {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|Q8191796|qid=Q8191796|1=Mizuhanome}} from Miyahara Suijin Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami|Q2842779|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|1=Takamimusubi}}, and {{ill|qid=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|qid=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|1=Kamimusubi}} from Morifusa Hayakawa Shrine are also enshrined together. === History === According to tradition, the shrine was established on August 6 in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258}}. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seiwa|Q349243|qid=Q349243|1=Emperor Seiwa}}, on June 25 of the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}}, it was elevated to an official shrine and granted the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. In ancient times, the entire area of the former Tazawa Village was designated as shrine land, but due to repeated warfare during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, these lands were abolished. According to the shrine’s on-site historical account, Afuku Kahaku Shrine was founded for flood control and irrigation of the Abukuma River and for the protection of the northern frontier of the Yamato court. At the time of its founding, it was located east of the present site, near the Abukuma River in Okuma Tazawa Aza Miyahara (below the embankment about 200 meters west of the Jōban Line tracks). It was later relocated to its current site atop Mount Suijō. The shrine was deeply revered by successive local lords. In the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}}, {{ill|lt=Date Shigenari|Q1038673|qid=Q1038673|1=Date Shigezane}}, the first head of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Watari Date clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, constructed shrine buildings, and later, in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}}, a donation of 12 koku and 6 to of rice was made. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, and honden) ** The main hall was built in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|qid=Q1327259|1=Ansei}} ({{ill|lt=1858|Q7684|qid=Q7684|1=1858}}) in the late Edo period. Owing to its fine carvings, it has been designated a cultural property of Watari Town. File:Torii gate of Afuku Kahaku-jinja shrine.JPG|Torii gate of Afuku Kahaku Shrine File:Afuku Kahaku-jinja shrine.JPG|Shrine precincts of Afuku Kahaku Shrine File:Precincts of Afuku-Kahaku-jinja shrine.JPG|Shrine buildings of Afuku Kahaku Shrine File:Honden of Afuku Kahaku-jinja shrine.JPG|Main hall of Afuku Kahaku Shrine (north side) File:Left side of Honden of Afuku Kahaku-jinja shrine.JPG|Main hall of Afuku Kahaku Shrine (south side) * In addition, to the south side of the main hall stands Koyasu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami}}), and within the precincts there is a mikoshi storehouse. File:Koyasu-jinja shrine of Afuku Kahaku-jinja shrine.JPG|Koyasu Shrine === Legends === Long ago, it is said that a god was making ridges ({{ill|lt=une|Q2262727|qid=Q2262727|1=Hilling}}) on a western mountain. Because seven ridges were made, the mountain came to be called Nanauneyama (“Seven Ridge Mountain”), and the remaining three handfuls of earth that were thrown aside formed a hill now known as Mount Suijō (“Three-Handful Mountain”), where Afuku Kahaku Shrine is presently located. There are also legends related to the {{ill|lt=Abukuma River|Q335853|qid=Q335853|1=Abukuma River}}. * One day, when the mountain god and the river god met, the mountain god said, “I have been building the mountains up to here, and I want to extend them further north,” while the river god said, “I want the river to flow toward the sea from around here.” Since neither would yield, they decided to follow the opinion of whoever could circle a nearby mountain called Miyama ten times the fastest. They began the race together, but as the mountain god entered the seventh lap, he was distracted by azaleas blooming on Miyama, tripped over their roots, and fell. While the mountain god was delayed by the fall, the river god pulled far ahead and won. Because of this, it is said that from around Afuku Kahaku Shrine, the Abukuma River changes its course eastward and flows toward the Pacific Ocean. It is also said that the mountain god is {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|Q386563|qid=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Sengan Shrine|Q63148000|qid=Q63148000|1=Sengan Shrine}} (formerly known as Miyama Daigongen), which is located in {{ill|lt=Iwanuma City|Q518203|qid=Q518203|1=Iwanuma}} on the opposite bank of Afuku Kahaku Shrine. === References === {{No footnotes|date=August 2020|section=1}} * “Watari District Shrine Register” (1899, held by Kawaguchi Shrine) * “Main Hall of Afuku Kahaku Shrine” (Watari Town Board of Education, March 2000) * “Furusato Watari: A Stroll Through History” (Watari Town, March 2009) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010280 Afuku Kahaku Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|qid=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{Shinto nav}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Afuku Kahaku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Afuku Kahaku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:亘理町の歴史]] [[Category:亘理町の建築物]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyagi Prefecture]]
